2605.12208.title: Self-Supervised Laplace Approximation for Bayesian Uncertainty Quantification
2605.12208.paper_summary: Approximate Bayesian inference typically revolves around computing the posterior parameter distribution. In practice, however, the main object of interest is often a model's predictions rather than its parameters. In this work, we propose to bypass the parameter posterior and focus directly on approximating the posterior predictive distribution. We achieve this by drawing inspiration from self-training within self-supervised and semi-supervised learning. Essentially, we quantify a Bayesian model's predictive uncertainty by refitting on self-predicted data. The idea is strikingly simple: If a model assigns high likelihood to self-predicted data, these predictions are of low uncertainty, and vice versa. This yields a deterministic, sampling-free approximation of the posterior predictive. The modular structure of our Self-Supervised Laplace Approximation (SSLA) further allows us to plug in different prior specifications, enabling classical Bayesian sensitivity (w.r.t. prior choice) analysis. In order to bypass expensive refitting, we further introduce an approximate version of SSLA, called ASSLA. We study (A)SSLA both theoretically and empirically in regression models ranging from Bayesian linear models to Bayesian neural networks. Across a wide array of regression tasks with simulated and real-world datasets, our methods outperform classical Laplace approximations in predictive calibration while remaining computationally efficient.
2605.12208.paper_summary_zh: 近似貝葉斯推斷通常圍繞計算後驗參數分佈。然而，在實踐中，主要的關注對象往往是模型的預測，而不是其參數。在這項工作中，我們提出繞過參數後驗，直接專注於近似後驗預測分佈。我們通過從自我訓練中獲取靈感，結合自我監督和半監督學習來實現這一點。本質上，我們通過在自我預測數據上重新擬合來量化貝葉斯模型的預測不確定性。這個想法非常簡單：如果模型對自我預測數據賦予高的可能性，則這些預測的不確定性低，反之亦然。這產生了一個確定性的、無需抽樣的後驗預測近似。我們的自我監督拉普拉斯近似（SSLA）的模塊化結構進一步允許我們插入不同的先驗規範，從而實現經典的貝葉斯敏感性（相對於先驗選擇）分析。為了繞過昂貴的重新擬合，我們進一步引入了一個近似版本的SSLA，稱為ASSLA。我們在理論和實證上研究（A）SSLA，涵蓋從貝葉斯線性模型到貝葉斯神經網絡的回歸模型。在多種回歸任務中，無論是模擬數據集還是真實世界數據集，我們的方法在預測校準方面超越了經典的拉普拉斯近似，同時保持計算效率。
2605.12208.author: Julian Rodemann et.al.
2605.12208.authors: Julian Rodemann, Alexander Marquard, Thomas Augustin, Michele Caprio
